input.thought: Open official Ramp documentation and first-party pages needed to verify auth, API surface, access gating, rate limits, and MCP availability.

arguments.task: Research official Ramp documentation and first-party pages. Visit docs.ramp.com and find pages that verify: available APIs and whether they are REST or GraphQL, authentication methods for the API, any rate limit documentation, and whether API access requires being a Ramp customer or other gating. Also look for official Ramp pages about developer platform access or pricing/access, and search official Ramp GitHub or official MCP registry pages for any Ramp MCP/server. Return the specific page URLs and short quoted findings only from the pages visited.

raw_result.description: Ramp provides a corporate finance platform with developer APIs for cards, accounting, reimbursements, and related spend operations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Ramp developer docs include an Authorization page and the browser task extracted OAuth 2.0 as the documented API auth method; no other auth method was verified from gathered evidence.
raw_result.gating_reason: The gathered official docs show a Sandbox page and imply access is tied to Ramp account/customer context, but the exact self-serve onboarding or approval flow was not fully verified from the collected evidence, so human review is required.

api_surface.breadth: Official developer docs expose a broad REST API surface with documented sections including cards, accounting, reimbursements, sandbox, authorization, rate limiting, and MCP-related developer resources.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Ramp docs include an MCP page mentioning three Ramp MCP servers, and the gathered evidence also identified an official-looking GitHub repository ramp-public/ramp_mcp. The exact scope and official status of all three servers should be human-verified.

raw_result.blocker: Public docs and OAuth-based API auth are present, but access gating appears tied to Ramp account/customer setup and was not fully verified as self-serve from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: The official rate limiting page was observed and the gathered evidence extracted a limit of 200 requests per 10-second rolling window.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs are publicly available at docs.ramp.com; browser-collected evidence indicates OAuth 2.0 auth, a documented sandbox, a published rate-limit page, and an MCP page listing Ramp MCP, Developer MCP, and Ramp Data MCP servers.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The existing evidence is sufficient to support REST, OAuth2, rate-limit, and MCP-exists findings, but the exact access tier for API use and the official status/scope of all MCP servers were not fully verified from first-party pages already gathered.
